sections.label: Section 992
sections.original: θεήλατον μάντευμα δεινόν , ὦ ξένε .
sections.translation: A divine oracle of dread import , stranger .

sections.label: Section 993
sections.original: ἦ ῥητόν ; ἢ οὐχὶ θεμιτὸν ἄλλον εἰδέναι ;
sections.translation: Proper , or improper , for another to know ?

sections.label: Section 994
sections.original: μάλιστά γʼ · εἶπε γάρ με Λοξίας ποτὲ
sections.translation: Proper , surely . Loxias once said that I was

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: χρῆναι μιγῆναι μητρὶ τἠμαυτοῦ τό τε
sections.translation: doomed to marry my own mother , and to shed with my own hands my father ’ s blood . For which reasons I long shirked my home in Corinth — with a happy outcome , to be sure , but still it is sweet to see the face of one ’ s parents .

sections.label: Section 999
sections.original: τὰ τῶν τεκόντων ὄμμαθʼ ἥδιστον βλέπειν .
sections.translation: Was it really for fear of this that you became an exile from that city ?

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: ἦ γὰρ τάδʼ ὀκνῶν κεῖθεν ἦσθʼ ἀπόπτολις ;
sections.translation: And because I did not wish , old man , to be the murderer of my father .

sections.label: Section 1001
sections.original: πατρός τε χρῄζων μὴ φονεὺς εἶναι , γέρον .
sections.translation: Why have I not relieved you of this second fear , my lord , since I came to give you pleasure ?

sections.label: Section 1002
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἐγὼ οὐχὶ τοῦδε τοῦ φόβου σʼ , ἄναξ ,
sections.translation: And indeed you will have worthy thanks from me .

sections.label: Section 1004
sections.original: καὶ μὴν χάριν γʼ ἂν ἀξίαν λάβοις ἐμοῦ .
sections.translation: And indeed I came specially for this , that

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: καὶ μὴν μάλιστα τοῦτʼ ἀφικόμην , ὅπως
sections.translation: I might profit from your returning home .

sections.label: Section 1007
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔποτʼ εἶμι τοῖς φυτεύσασίν γʼ ὅμοῦ .
sections.translation: But by no means will I ever go near my parents again .

sections.label: Section 1008
sections.original: ὦ παῖ , καλῶς εἶ δῆλος οὐκ εἰδὼς τί δρᾷς .
sections.translation: My son , it is crystal clear that you do not know what you are doing .

sections.label: Section 1009
sections.original: πῶς , ὦ γεραιέ ; πρὸς θεῶν δίδασκέ με .
sections.translation: How so , old man ? In the name of the gods , tell me .

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: εἰ τῶνδε φεύγεις οὕνεκʼ εἰς οἴκους μολεῖν .
sections.translation: If on account of this you are fleeing from returning home .

sections.label: Section 1011
sections.original: ταρβῶν γε μή μοι Φοῖβος ἐξέλθῃ σαφής .
sections.translation: Fearing indeed lest Apollo ’ s prophecy come true in me .

sections.label: Section 1012
sections.original: ἦ μὴ μίασμα τῶν φυτευσάντων λάβῃς ;
sections.translation: Lest you acquire some pollution from your parents ?

sections.label: Section 1013
sections.original: τοῦτʼ αὐτό , πρέσβυ , τοῦτό μʼ εἰσαεὶ φοβεῖ .
sections.translation: This very thing , old man , this constantly frightens me .

sections.label: Section 1014
sections.original: ἆρʼ οἶσθα δῆτα πρὸς δίκης οὐδὲν τρέμων ;
sections.translation: Do you know , then , that your fears are wholly in vain ?

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: πῶς δʼ οὐχί , παῖς γʼ εἰ τῶνδε γεννητῶν ἔφυν ;
sections.translation: ⋯ How so , if I was born of those parents ?

sections.label: Section 1016
sections.original: ὁθούνεκʼ ἦν σοι Πόλυβος οὐδὲν ἐν γένει .
sections.translation: Because you had no blood in common with Polybus .

sections.label: Section 1017
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας ; οὐ γὰρ Πόλυβος ἐξέφυσέ με ;
sections.translation: What are you saying ? Was Polybus not my father ?

sections.label: Section 1018
sections.original: οὐ μᾶλλον οὐδὲν τοῦδε τἀνδρός , ἀλλʼ ἴσον .
sections.translation: Just as much , and no more , than he who speaks to you .

sections.label: Section 1019
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ὁ φύσας ἐξ ἴσου τῷ μηδενί ;
sections.translation: And how can my father be equal to him who is as though nothing to me ?

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔ σʼ ἐγείνατʼ οὔτʼ ἐκεῖνος οὔτʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: But he did not father you , any more than I did .

sections.label: Section 1021
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἀντὶ τοῦ δὴ παῖδά μʼ ὠνομάζετο ;
sections.translation: How , then , did he call me his son ?

sections.label: Section 1022
sections.original: δῶρόν ποτʼ , ἴσθι , τῶν ἐμῶν χειρῶν λαβών .
sections.translation: Long ago he received you as a gift from my hands .

sections.label: Section 1023
sections.original: κᾆθʼ ὧδʼ ἀπʼ ἄλλης χειρὸς ἔστερξεν μέγα ;
sections.translation: And yet he loved me so dearly , who came from another ’ s hand ?

sections.label: Section 1024
sections.original: ἡ γὰρ πρὶν αὐτὸν ἐξέπεισʼ ἀπαιδία .
sections.translation: His former childlessness won him over .

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἐμπολήσας ἢ τυχών μʼ αὐτῷ δίδως ;
sections.translation: And had you bought me or found me by chance , when you gave me to him ?

sections.label: Section 1026
sections.original: εὑρὼν ναπαίαις ἐν Κιθαιρῶνος πτυχαῖς .
sections.translation: I found you in Cithaeron ’ s winding glens .

sections.label: Section 1027
sections.original: ὡδοιπόρεις δὲ πρὸς τί τούσδε τοὺς τόπους ;
sections.translation: And why were you roaming those regions ?

sections.label: Section 1028
sections.original: ἐνταῦθʼ ὀρείοις ποιμνίοις ἐπεστάτουν .
sections.translation: I was in charge of mountain flocks .

sections.label: Section 1029
sections.original: ποιμὴν γὰρ ἦσθα κἀπὶ θητείᾳ πλάνης ;
sections.translation: What , you were a shepherd — a vagrant hireling ?

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: σοῦ τʼ , ὦ τέκνον , σωτήρ γε τῷ τότʼ ἐν χρόνῳ .
sections.translation: But your savior , my son , in that hour .

sections.label: Section 1031
sections.original: τί δʼ ἄλγος ἴσχοντʼ ἀγκάλαις με λαμβάνεις ;
sections.translation: And what was my pain when you took me in your arms ?

sections.label: Section 1032
sections.original: ποδῶν ἂν ἄρθρα μαρτυρήσειεν τὰ σά ·
sections.translation: The ankles of your feet might bear witness .

sections.label: Section 1033
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί τοῦτʼ ἀρχαῖον ἐννέπεις κακόν ;
sections.translation: Ah me , why do you speak of that old trouble ?

sections.label: Section 1034
sections.original: λύω σʼ ἔχοντα διατόρους ποδοῖν ἀκμάς .
sections.translation: I freed you when you had your ankles pinned together .

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: δεινόν γʼ ὄνειδος σπαργάνων ἀνειλόμην .
sections.translation: It was a dread brand of shame that I took from my cradle .

sections.label: Section 1036
sections.original: ὥστʼ ὠνομάσθης ἐκ τύχης ταύτης ὃς εἶ .
sections.translation: So much so that from that fortune you were called by that name which you still bear .

sections.label: Section 1037
sections.original: ὦ πρὸς θεῶν , πρὸς μητρὸς ἢ πατρός ; φράσον .
sections.translation: Oh , in the name of the gods , was the deed my mother ’ s or father ’ s ? Speak !

sections.label: Section 1038
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ · ὁ δοὺς δὲ ταῦτʼ ἐμοῦ λῷον φρονεῖ .
sections.translation: I know not . He who gave you to me knows better of that than I .

sections.label: Section 1039
sections.original: ἦ γὰρ παρʼ ἄλλου μʼ ἔλαβες οὐδʼ αὐτὸς τυχών ;
sections.translation: What , you got me from another ? You did not find me yourself ?

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ ποιμὴν ἄλλος ἐκδίδωσί μοι .
sections.translation: No . Another shepherd gave you to me .

sections.label: Section 1041
sections.original: τίς οὗτος ; ἦ κάτοισθα δηλῶσαι λόγῳ ;
sections.translation: Who was he ? Can you tell clearly ?

sections.label: Section 1042
sections.original: τῶν Λαΐου δήπου τις ὠνομάζετο .
sections.translation: I think he was said to be one of the household of Laius .

sections.label: Section 1043
sections.original: ἦ τοῦ τυράννου τῆσδε γῆς πάλαι ποτέ ;
sections.translation: The king who ruled this country long ago ?

sections.label: Section 1044
sections.original: μάλιστα · τούτου τἀνδρὸς οὗτος ἦν βοτήρ .
sections.translation: The same . The man was a herdsman in his service .

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: ἦ κἄστʼ ἔτι ζῶν οὗτος , ὥστʼ ἰδεῖν ἐμέ ;
sections.translation: Is he still alive , that I might see him ?

sections.label: Section 1046
sections.original: ὑμεῖς γʼ ἄριστʼ εἰδεῖτʼ ἂν οὑπιχώριοι .
sections.translation: You natives of this country should know best .

sections.label: Section 1047
sections.original: ἔστιν τις ὑμῶν τῶν παρεστώτων πέλας ,
sections.translation: Is there any of you here present that knows the herdsman of whom he speaks , having seen him either in the pastures or here in town ? Answer !

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: σημήναθʼ , ὡς ὁ καιρὸς ηὑρῆσθαι τάδε .
sections.translation: The hour has come for these things to be revealed finally .

sections.label: Section 1051
sections.original: οἶμαι μὲν οὐδένʼ ἄλλον ἢ τὸν ἐξ ἀγρῶν ,
sections.translation: I think he speaks of no other than the peasant you already wanted to see . But our lady Iocasta might best tell you that .

sections.label: Section 1054
sections.original: γύναι , νοεῖς ἐκεῖνον , ὅντινʼ ἀρτίως
sections.translation: Lady , do you know the one whom we summoned just now ?

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: μολεῖν ἐφιέμεσθα ; τόνδʼ οὗτος λέγει ;
sections.translation: Is it of him that this man speaks ?

sections.label: Section 1056
sections.original: τί δʼ ὅντινʼ εἶπε ; μηδὲν ἐντραπῇς · τὰ δὲ
sections.translation: Why ask of whom he spoke ? Regard it not ; waste not a thought on what he said ; it would be vain .

sections.label: Section 1058
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν γένοιτο τοῦθʼ ὅπως ἐγὼ λαβὼν
sections.translation: It must not happen that , with such clues in my grasp , I fail to bring my birth to light .

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: μὴ πρὸς θεῶν , εἴπερ τι τοῦ σαυτοῦ βίου
sections.translation: For the gods ’ sake , if you have any care for your own life , do not continue this search ! My anguish is enough .

sections.label: Section 1062
sections.original: θάρσει · σὺ μὲν γὰρ οὐδʼ ἐὰν τρίτης ἐγὼ
sections.translation: Be of good courage . Even if I should be found the son of a servile mother — a slave by three descents — you will not be proven baseborn .

sections.label: Section 1064
sections.original: ὅμως πιθοῦ μοι , λίσσομαι · μὴ δρᾶ τάδε .
sections.translation: Hear me , I implore you : do not do this .
